sourceMovie.title: Chappie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Chappie works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chappie clicked for you, The Terminator (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chappie clicked for you, Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Chappie?
faq.answer: Try The Terminator, Terminator 2: Judgment Day, A Clockwork Orange—each shares DNA with Chappie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Chappie?
faq.answer: Chappie (2015) is best described as Crime, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Chappie worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
